title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram

content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers representing heights of bars in a histogram, find the area of the largest rectangle that can be formed by these bars. Each bar has width 1.


Input Format

First line contains T, number of test cases
Each test case contains two lines of input

First line contains N, number of bars
Second line contains N space-separated integers representing heights of bars




Output Format

For each test case, print the area of the largest possible rectangle.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of bars in the histogram; the second line contains N space-separated integers h₁, h₂, …, h_N representing the heights of the bars.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible rectangular area that can be formed using consecutive bars of the histogram.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: the maximal rectangle uses bars 3 and 4 (heights 5 and 6) with height 5 and width 2, area 10. Test case 2: all bars have height 2, width 4, area 8.
Test case 1: rectangle of height 1 covering all three bars gives area 3. Test case 2: rectangle of height 3 covering bars 3,4,5 gives area 9.
All bars have height 1, width 5, so the largest rectangle area is 5.

solution.approach: The classic solution uses a monotonic increasing stack that stores indices of bars. While scanning the histogram, when a bar lower than the bar at the stack top is encountered, we pop the stack and compute the area with the popped bar as the smallest height. The width is determined by the current index and the new stack top. After processing all bars, we pop remaining bars to compute areas extending to the end. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) auxiliary space for the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the stack.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i is test case index from 0 to 20 inclusive.
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
        print(0)
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
        print(5)
    elif i == 2:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 2
        print(n)
        print('0 0')
    elif i == 3:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 2
        print(n)
        print('1 0')
    elif i == 4:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(['1000000000']*15))
    elif i == 5:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(str(x) for x in range(15,0,-1)))
    elif i == 6:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(str(x) for x in range(1,16)))
    elif i == 7:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        arr = []
        for j in range(15):
            arr.append('1000000000' if j%2==0 else '0')
        print(' '.join(arr))
    elif i == 8:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 10
        print(n)
        print(' '.join(['7']*10))
    elif i == 9:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print('0 0 0 0 0')
    elif i == 10:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 7
        print(n)
        print('1 2 3 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 11:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 7
        print(n)
        print('5 4 3 2 3 4 5')
    elif i == 12:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        arr = ['1','1','1','1000000000'] + ['1']*11
        print(' '.join(arr))
    elif i == 13:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 7
        print(n)
        print('2 3 5 7 11 13 17')
    elif i == 14:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 5
        print(n)
        print('5 5 5 5 1')
    elif i == 15:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 8
        print(n)
        print('0 5 0 5 0 5 0 5')
    elif i == 16:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(3)
        print('2 1 2')
        print(4)
        print('4 4 4 4')
        print(5)
        print('5 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 17:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print(1)
        print(0)
        print(15)
        print('3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6 5 3 5 8 9 7 9')
    elif i == 18:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for val in range(1,6):
            print(1)
            print(val)
    elif i == 19:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 15
        print(n)
        print('9 7 5 3 1 2 4 6 8 10 12 14 13 11 9')
    else:
        # default empty case
        print(0)
